name: Fathia Royal Cloth
size: 6 yards · full men’s toga / 12 yards women’s set
desc: A commanding cloth in the classic red, gold, green and blue — the colours of celebration and royalty. Woven strip by strip and hand-joined so the zig-zag motifs align across the full width.

name: Emaa Da Set
desc: A softer palette of olive green, gold and rose — refined and modern while staying true to the traditional weave. A favourite for engagements and outdooring ceremonies.

name: Nsroma Cloth
desc: Vivid magenta, emerald and orange charged with shimmer thread. A statement piece that catches the light — woven for those who want to be seen.

name: Graduation Stole
desc: A handwoven kente stole to crown your milestone. Rich, durable and made to be kept — a graduation keepsake passed down for years.

name: Woven Clutch Bag
desc: A structured clutch cut from genuine kente and fully lined. The perfect finishing touch for weddings, church and events.

name: Kente Bow Tie
desc: A self-tie kente bow tie for grooms, graduates and gentlemen. Small in size, big on statement.

name: Kente Table Runner
desc: Bring the warmth of Bonwire to your table. A handwoven runner that turns any gathering into a celebration.

name: Kente Head Wrap
desc: A generous head wrap (duku) in vibrant kente — tie it your way. Also worn as a shawl or sash.

name: Ahenkyew Royal Cloth
desc: handwoven full piece men cloth with rich colors to depict royalty.

name: Royal Wedding Kente Cloth
meaning: woven for the men of the day
desc: Some days call for more than a suit. Woven in royal blue, gold, forest green and deep red, this kente is made for the men who stand at the center of it all — the groom taking his vows, the father walking his daughter down the aisle, and the father of the groom seated proudly in the front row. Every strip is hand-loomed and joined by hand so the pattern falls perfectly across the shoulders, the same way it has for generations of Ashanti men before you. Wear it once and it becomes part of your story — a cloth your sons and daughters will point to one day and say, "that's what Dad wore."
